query: Wang Yi China Foreign Minister visit South Korea 2026 postponed Taiwan dispute rescheduled July August 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Wang Yi's Postponed South Korea Visit — Taiwan Labeling Dispute (2026)

**The core facts:**
- Chinese FM Wang Yi's planned visit to South Korea was **put on hold in April 2026** over a Taiwan labeling dispute in Seoul's electronic arrival (e-arrival) card system.
- **Important nuance:** The postponement was triggered by Seoul **removing** the "China (Taiwan)" label (to comply with Taipei's demands) on March 31, 2026 — which angered *Beijing*, not the reverse. China objected because it saw this as inconsistent with its "one China" principle.

**Timeline:**
- Feb 2025: Seoul added "China (Taiwan)" label → Taiwan protested
- Mar 31, 2026: Seoul announced removal of the departure/destination fields entirely
- Apr 8–10, 2026: Wang visited North Korea; Seoul hoped he'd stop in Seoul afterward
- Apr 11, 2026: Card change took effect
- Apr 14, 2026: Beijing reiterated "Taiwan is part of China"
- Apr 20, 2026: Media confirmed the visit was on hold; Seoul's MOFA publicly denied a formal delay, calling the card change "administrative"
- Apr 21, 2026: China also reportedly denied entry to South Korea's intelligence chief
- Jul 2–8, 2026: Wang visited Nordic Europe instead — **no Seoul visit rescheduled**

**On the "July/August 2026 rescheduled" part of your question:** I found **no evidence** of a confirmed rescheduling to July or August 2026. As of early July 2026, the trip remained unscheduled with no concrete dates exchanged, and Wang Yi's July itinerary was in Denmark, Sweden, Finland, and Norway.

The full structured findings (key facts, sources, and data points) have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Wang Yi (politician)

key_facts: China put Chinese FM Wang Yi's planned visit to South Korea on hold in April 2026 over a dispute about Taiwan's labeling in Seoul's electronic arrival card system.

The trigger was Seoul's March 31, 2026 decision to REMOVE the 'China (Taiwan)' label (by deleting departure/destination fields) to comply with Taiwan's demands, which angered Beijing — not a labeling that pleased China.
The e-arrival card change took effect April 11, 2026; South Korea removed the 'previous departure place' and 'next destination' fields for all global travelers.
Wang Yi visited North Korea (Pyongyang) April 8-10, 2026, meeting FM Choe Son-hui and Kim Jong-un; Seoul had hoped he would visit Seoul immediately afterward but he did not.

Seoul's Foreign Ministry publicly denied on April 20, 2026 that the visit was formally delayed, calling the card change 'a simple administrative and technical adjustment' and saying 'no details finalized.'
A Chinese MOFA spokesperson stated on April 14, 2026 that 'Taiwan is part of China' and labeling it 'China (Taiwan)' is 'only right/entirely natural,' signaling dissatisfaction.
Seoul sought Wang's visit as a follow-up to Lee Jae-myung–Xi Jinping summits (Nov 2025, Jan 2026) and ahead of an expected Trump–Xi summit (reportedly mid-May 2026).
As of early July 2026, no rescheduled Seoul visit date was announced; Wang Yi instead visited Denmark, Sweden, Finland, and Norway July 2-8, 2026.
On April 21, 2026, China reportedly also denied entry to South Korea's intelligence (NIS) chief amid the Taiwan tensions.
On July 7, 2026, South Korea's FM Cho Hyun met US Secretary Rubio and Japan's FM Motegi in Ankara, reaffirming Taiwan Strait stability.
